summary:
Israel launched strikes on Tehran and Hezbollah targets in Lebanon on Tuesday. Iran fired missiles at the UAE and Qatar, which intercepted them over Dubai and Doha, closing airspace briefly. Gulf residents and travelers faced disruptions; energy supplies face risks from the escalation. This follows Iranian missile launches at Israel last week. Further strikes are expected between the parties.

A Pakistani airstrike hit a Kabul drug rehabilitation hospital late Monday, killing 400 people. Afghanistan accuses Pakistan amid cross-border clashes since last month; residents and Taliban police are affected. Ceasefire calls have intensified. This follows Pakistani airstrikes last week that killed 75 civilians and displaced 115,000 in Afghanistan. In eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, Rwanda-backed rebels entered Goma city after overrunning troops, displacing over 1 million people inside the city per UN estimates; aid supplies are strained. This follows rebels overrunning Goma-area troops last week.

In northeastern Nigeria, suicide bombers attacked three sites in Borno state Monday night, killing scores and injuring others in Maiduguri and surroundings; emergency services are responding. South Sudan army ordered 50,000 Jonglei state residents to evacuate ahead of an offensive against rebels; displacement is imminent. Cuba's electrical grid failed island-wide, affecting all 11 million residents, after US blocked Venezuelan oil shipments; the government is investigating. Separately, a US appeals court lifted a block on Trump executive orders ending federal DEI programs; enforcement resumes in agencies. UK ended its Global Health Workforce Program in six African countries due to aid cuts, affecting health workers and patients this month. Chile's President Kast started northern border barrier construction against illegal immigration. SpaceX launched Crew-10 from Kennedy Space Center Friday with NASA, Japan, and Russia astronauts to relieve the ISS crew stuck nine months; Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams return soon. NATO deployed its first alliance-wide response after a Baltic Sea cable severance in a suspected sabotage campaign; patrols have increased.

headlines.title: Pakistani Airstrike Kills 400 People at Kabul Drug Rehabilitation Hospital
headlines.summary: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of striking a Kabul hospital treating drug users late Monday, killing 400 amid escalating cross-border clashes since last month. Residents and Taliban police affected; ceasefire calls intensify. Raises risks of broader South Asian conflict.

framings.angle: Accused Pakistan of targeting hospital killing 400 in dramatic escalation of recent clashes
Covered Afghanistan accusation of Pakistan hospital airstrike with Pakistan dismissal
Visited struck facility and observed more than 30 bodies carried out on stretchers

headlines.title: Israel Launches Strikes on Tehran and Lebanon as Iran Fires Missiles at UAE Qatar
headlines.summary: Israel strikes Tehran and Hezbollah targets in Lebanon while UAE intercepts Iranian missiles over Dubai and Doha, closing airspace briefly. Gulf residents and travelers disrupted; energy supplies at risk. Escalates Middle East war threatening global markets.

framings.angle: Highlighted explosions in Dubai Doha from Iranian fire and Israel new strikes Tuesday
Live updated UAE intercepts of Iranian missiles drones and Israel strikes on Tehran Lebanon

headlines.title: Cuba Endures Island-Wide Blackout Amid Trump Oil Blockade on Venezuelan Shipments
headlines.summary: Cuban electrical grid disconnects completely causing nationwide blackout as US blocks Venezuelan oil to island. All 11 million Cubans lose power; government investigates. Exacerbates economic crisis prompting signals of foreign investment openness.

headlines.title: Suicide Bombers Kill Scores of People in Three Borno State Locations Nigeria
headlines.summary: Suicide bombers attack at least three sites in northeastern Nigeria's Borno state Monday night leaving scores dead and injured. Residents of Maiduguri and surroundings affected; emergency services respond. Signals persistent Boko Haram threat to regional stability.

headlines.title: South Sudan Army Orders 50,000 Jonglei Residents to Flee Ahead of Offensive
headlines.summary: South Sudan army warns 50,000 people to evacuate homes in Jonglei state before major military operation against rebels. Civilians on brink of civil war affected; displacement imminent. Threatens renewed nationwide conflict after fragile peace.

headlines.title: US Appeals Court Lifts Block on Trump Administration DEI Crackdown Orders
headlines.summary: Fourth Circuit appeals court allows enforcement of Trump executive orders ending federal DEI programs overturning lower court block. Government agencies and employees impacted; implementation resumes. Advances conservative policy reshaping workplace diversity initiatives.

headlines.title: UK Government Cancels Global Health Workforce Program in Six African Countries
headlines.summary: UK axes flagship GHWP supporting healthcare training in six African nations due to aid budget cuts. Health workers and patients in Africa affected; program ends this month. Undermines pandemic preparedness hailed as UK security protection.

headlines.title: Chile President Kast Launches Northern Border Barrier Plan to Curb Immigration
headlines.summary: Chile's new far-right president begins fortified barrier construction along northern border against illegal immigration. Northern communities and migrants impacted; reflects rising crime concerns. Signals tougher regional migration controls amid public demand.

headlines.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Goma City Displacing Over 1 Million Congolese
headlines.summary: Rwanda-backed rebels overrun troops and enter Goma safe-haven city triggering gunfire and shelling. Over 1 million displaced inside city per UN; aid strained. Worsens eastern Congo humanitarian catastrophe.
headlines.previousContext: Rebels advance from overrunning troops into Goma safe-haven city raising displacement to 1 million

headlines.title: NASA SpaceX Launches Crew-10 Rocket to Retrieve Astronauts Stuck Nine Months
headlines.summary: SpaceX Falcon 9 carries Crew-10 with NASA Japan Russia astronauts from Kennedy Space Center Friday to relieve station crew. Butch Wilmore Suni Williams return soon after nine months. Ensures continuous ISS operations vital for space research.
headlines.previousContext: Crew-10 Falcon 9 rocket lifts off Friday evening docking expected Saturday

headlines.title: NATO Deploys Coordinated Response to Suspected Baltic Sea Cable Sabotage
headlines.summary: NATO activates first alliance-wide response after another deep-sea cable severed in suspected sabotage campaign. Baltic infrastructure and members affected; patrols increase. Protects critical undersea communications amid hybrid threats.
headlines.previousContext: Alliance mounts first coordinated operation following second cable severance
